title: Yard Person
description:
Yard Person
3535 John Capron Road, Lynchburg, VA, 24501, United States
Job Category
Asphalt Plant
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Required Degree
NONE
Minimum Experience
1 Year
Description
The Loader Operator ensures the safe loading of materials into trucks and feeder bins.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Clearing and cleaning of the yard by removing litter, debris, rocks, asphalt, bin
materials, etc.
● Mowing, trimming and maintenance of the lawn areas as required.
● Maintain cleanliness of the parking area
● Give direction to suppliers to ensuring loads of liquid and fuel get into the proper
tank.
● Grease and inspect the plant to identify and communicate problem areas.
● Other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● Must be able to lift 50 lbs regularly throughout a shift
● Ability to learn how to use and handle hand tools including a rake, shovel and air
powered and hand pumps.
● Manuel dexterity to safely use tools and equipment.
● Good eye-hand coordination, good physical fitness, multi-limb coordination, static
strength and a good sense of balance.
● Ability to perform strenuous or peak physical activities for prolonged periods of
time under conditions of various weather conditions and extreme environments.
● Ability to drive a Bobcat and operate lawn equipment.
● Ability to adhere to Superior policies and other safety regulations.
● Ability to follow verbal and written instructions.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as required.
Core Responsibilities
To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following
competencies:
● Attendance/Punctuality - is consistently at work and on time; ensures work
responsibilities are covered when absent; arrives at meetings and appointments
on time.
● Safety and Security - observes safety and security procedures; reports potentially
unsafe conditions; uses equipment and materials properly.
● Teamwork - contributes to building a positive team spirit; puts success of team
above own interests.
● Quality - demonstrates accuracy and thoroughness; monitors own work to ensure
quality.
● Productivity/Quantity - meets productivity standards; completes work in timely
manner; strives to increase productivity; works quickly.
● Dependability - follows instructions, responds to management directions;
commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals; completes tasks
on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan.
● Communication - listens and gets clarification; responds well to questions.
Physical Demands
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by
an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable
accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the
essential functions.
Essential functions may require maintaining physical condition necessary for standing,
walking, bending, stooping, pushing, pulling, twisting, grasping; and seeing and hearing
within normal ranges. Must lift and/or move up to 50 pounds unassisted throughout the
hour and shift.
Work Environment/Tools and Equipment
The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee
encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be
made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
Vehicle and field environment; travel from site to site; exposure to noise, dust, fumes, vibrations;
temperature variations and inclement weather conditions; work around heavy construction equipment.
Work on uneven or wet surfaces.
Tools and equipment regularly used; bobcat, lawn mower, riding mower, trimmer, shovel, rake, broom,
water hose, air pump, hand pump, jackhammer, Bobcat, and a variety of hand tools

title: Accounting Assistant
description:
Accounting Assistant
5551 Wellington Road, Gainesville, VA, 20155, United States
Job Category
Accounting
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Required Degree
High school
Description
We are seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Accounting Assistant to join
our team. The ideal candidate is organized, comfortable working with numbers, and
able to effectively communicate with vendors, drivers, and plant personnel. The role will
primarily focus on coding and entering haul invoices, tracking stocked material
quantities, and assisting with daily cash sale deposits.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Assist with the coding and processing of various invoices, including job haul and
material delivery invoices, ensuring accurate and timely entry into the accounting
system according to job cost and chart of accounts
● Track material deliveries (stone, liquid asphalt, etc.) verify against invoices.
● Assist with preparing and making daily bank deposits, including logging and
reconciling checks and cash.
● Assist with reconciliation and posting of AR, credit card payments and cash
receipts into the accounting system
● Maintain organized records of invoices, delivery tickets, and deposits, both
digitally and in paper files.
● Support the accounting department with other general clerical duties such as
data entry, filing, and document scanning.
● Provide support to accounting department for other processes during month end
close
● Provide coverage of front desk during lunch breaks
● Other duties as assigned
Qualifications
● High school diploma or equivalent required; Associate’s degree in accounting or
business is a plus.
● 2-3 years relevant experience preferred.
● Experience in the construction industry is a plus..
● Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
● Ability to work independently and in a team environment.
● Strong organizational abilities with a keen eye for detail.
● Proficiency in office software (e.g., Microsoft Office Suite).
● Ability to multitask and prioritize in a dynamic work environment.
Physical Demands
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by
an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable
accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the
essential functions.
Essential functions may require maintaining physical condition necessary for standing,
walking, bending, stooping, seeing and hearing within normal ranges.  Job requires
ability to sit for long periods of time with minimal breaks.
Must be able to lift and/or move up to 20 pounds unassisted.
Work Environment/Tools and Equipment
The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an
employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable
accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the
essential functions.
While performing the duties of this job, the employee’s work is located primarily indoors.
Tools and equipment regularly used; computer, office supplies, copiers, telephone,
calculator, etc.

title: Asphalt Field QA Technician
description:
Asphalt Field QA Technician
27608 Gum Spring Road, Chantilly, VA, 20152, United States
Job Category
Asphalt, Quality Assurance
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Description
This position works in the field monitoring and testing product to ensure proper density
requirements are met.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Perform density testing at multiple sites throughout central and northern Virginia.
● Complete and submit testing records manually and /or electronically according to
Superior requirements.
● Communicate density testing results to Foreman, Lab Technician and Supervisor
according to established standards.
● Monitor all Superior Paving jobs.
● Place temporary pavement dotting on all state jobs.
● Assist paving crews in shoveling, raking, rolling, digging, spreading, leveling
asphalt, setting stakes, string lining, cleaning paving areas, monitoring
equipment, traffic control, etc. in accordance with established standards.
● Other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● Ability to follow verbal and written instructions and procedures.
● Ability to perform accurate computations (addition, subtraction, multiplication,
division, fractions, units of measure, and decimals).
● Ability to obtain VDOT asphalt field certification required.
● Must obtain be able to obtain Nuclear Safety Certification within one month.
● Must be able to read, write, speak English, and record testing results.
● Must be able to lift 50 lbs unassisted regularly throughout a shift.
● Must be able to stand for 12 hours and be able to walk up to 4 miles in a day with
minimal breaks.
● Good eye-hand coordination, good physical fitness, multi-limb coordination, static
strength and a good sense of balance.
● Ability to perform strenuous or peak physical activities under conditions of
various weather conditions and extreme environments.
● Ability to work as a member of a team.
● Ability to adhere to Superior policies and other safety regulations.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as required.
● Valid Driver’s License with a satisfactory driving record.
● High School Diploma or equivalency required.
● Experience with asphalt emulsion is a plus.
● General knowledge of computers, MS Office, including Word, Excel and
PowerPoint is a plus.
Core Responsibilities
To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following
competencies:
● Attendance/Punctuality - is consistently at work and on time; ensures work
responsibilities are covered when absent; arrives at meetings and appointments
on time.
● Safety and Security - observes safety and security procedures; reports potentially
unsafe conditions; uses equipment and materials properly.
● Teamwork - contributes to building a positive team spirit; puts success of team
above own interests.
● Quality - demonstrates accuracy and thoroughness; monitors own work to ensure
quality.
● Productivity/Quantity - meets productivity standards; completes work in timely
manner; strives to increase productivity; works quickly.
● Dependability - follows instructions, responds to management directions;
commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals; completes tasks
on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan.
● Communication - listens and gets clarification; responds well to questions.

title: Inventory Specialist
description:
Inventory Specialist
1678 Lawyers Road, Lynchburg, VA, 24501, United States
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Manage Others
No
Minimum Experience
2 Years
Description
The Parts Specialist is responsible for locating, analyzing, purchasing, receiving, and
tracking all parts, supplies, and equipment necessary for maintenance and operations.
This role ensures timely availability of materials, maintains accurate inventory levels,
and supports cost control by using a Computerized Maintenance Management System
(CMMS) to allocate expenses to the appropriate accounts and equipment. The ideal
candidate is organized, detail-oriented, and effective at coordinating with vendors,
maintenance staff, and other departments to support smooth and efficient operations.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Locate and maintain sources for equipment parts, supplies and materials.
● Perform daily activity in a manner that ensures timely location and order of parts
using the designated computer tracking system.
● Perform accurate and timely computer transactions including parts issues, stock
returns to ensure inventory integrity is maintained.
● Receive, count, and inspect purchased items to ensure accurate quantity and
quality.
● Deliver parts and/or company materials to designated locations.
● Process purchase requisitions and purchase orders in a timely manner.
● Prepare informal quotes and evaluate quotes and parts/equipment purchases.
● Do periodic inventory counts of parts room items to ensure proper identification,
re-supply needs and proper inventory counts.
● Perform shipping functions in accordance with company and regulatory shipping
standards.
● Maintain a clean and orderly stockroom in accordance with company guidelines.
● Success in this role requires the ability to effectively prioritize time and tasks in a
fast-paced environment
● Maintain clear and effective communication with vendors, maintenance teams,
and supervisors to ensure timely availability and delivery of parts.
● Manage multiple tasks simultaneously, including processing orders, coordinating
deliveries, and maintaining accurate records.
● Identify and resolve inventory or procurement issues quickly by applying strong
problem-solving skills.
● Data entry.
● Data analysis
● Perform other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● 2 years experience.
● Heavy lifting required.
● Computer Knowledge Required
● Must be comfortable driving an F-250 size truck.
● Operate a forklift and pallet jack.
● Interact with mechanics at a technical level.
● Use parts catalogs to identify correct parts to be ordered.
● Flexible - schedule is subject to change.
● A good understanding of state, local and private requirements and regulations.
● Ability to work overtime and weekend as necessary.
● Team player - work well with others.
● Self-motivated - be able to work without supervision.
● Detail oriented / quick learner.
● Work under pressure ability to evaluate and solve problems.
● Valid drivers license with satisfactory record.
● High school diploma or equivalency preferred.
● Ability to read, write and speak English.

responsibilities are covered when absent; arrives at meetings and appointments
on time.

title: Service/ Fuel and Lube Truck Driver
description:
Service/ Fuel and Lube Truck Driver
5551 Wellington Rd, Gainesville, VA, 20155, United States
Job Category
Equipment Operator, Paving Crew, Asphalt
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Description
The Service Truck Operator supports asphalt paving operations by operating and
maintaining a service truck used to deliver materials, tools, and support equipment to
field crews. This role ensures equipment readiness, performs routine maintenance,
handles hazardous materials safely, and assists paving crews when not driving. The
operator plays a key role in keeping paving operations efficient, safe, and productive.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Operates and maintains the service truck to support asphalt paving operations by
safely transporting materials, tools, and equipment to job sites.
● Performs daily equipment inspections, identifies and reports mechanical issues,
and carries out routine preventive maintenance while monitoring fluid systems
each day and servicing or emptying them as needed to ensure proper and
reliable operation.
● Ensures the truck is fueled and fully stocked and keeps all systems in proper
working order to minimize downtime and keep paving crews supplied and
productive.
● Maintains a clean, organized, and audit-ready service truck at all times.
● Monitor material levels, spray patterns, and application rates to maintain
consistent quality
● Follows proper labeling, containment, spill-prevention, and cleanup procedures
for hazardous materials.
● Safely handles, stores, transports, and dispenses hazardous materials in
compliance with OSHA, DOT, EPA, and company regulations. Follow all traffic
control procedures, safety guidelines, and environmental regulations
● Maintain accurate logs, documentation, and daily reports as required
● Maintains a clean, safe, and compliant work environment by following all
company safety policies, adhering to OSHA regulations, and practicing safe
driving and equipment-handling procedures at all times.
● Assist with jobsite setup, including layout, traffic control, and staging of materials
when not driving.
● Other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● Valid CDL type B with HazMat and tank endorsement required.
● Strong understanding of safe driving practices in active construction zones.
● 1-2 years of experience operating commercial vehicles in paving, milling, or
heavy construction environments preferred.
● Knowledge of basic mechanical systems and preventive maintenance.
● Reliable transportation.
● Consistently demonstrate the organization’s core values by striving for
excellence, fostering partnership, upholding integrity, and showing commitment.
● Ability to follow verbal instructions and work as part of a team.
● Must maintain constant situational awareness of moving vehicles, heavy
machinery, and other hazards inherent to paving and construction operations
● Ability to adhere to Superior policies and other safety regulations.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as required.
● Must be 18 years of age
Physical Demands
This position requires the ability to lift and carry up to 75 pounds; pull up one’s own
body weight; and sit in one position for up to 12 hours per day. It also requires the ability
to stand, walk, bend, kneel, and perform repetitive motions for extended periods;
tolerate noise, vibration, heat, and outdoor conditions common to paving operations;
demonstrate the manual dexterity needed to use hand tools and equipment; and
maintain good eye-hand coordination, physical fitness, multi-limb coordination, static
strength, and balance.
Work Environment/Tools and Equipment
Work is performed outdoors on roadways, construction zones, and active traffic areas,
often in hot, cold, or otherwise adverse weather. Employees are regularly exposed to
hot asphalt, dust, fumes, loud noise, and vibration from tools and equipment.
The physical demands and work environment described here are representative of
those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions
of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with
disabilities to perform the essential functions, in accordance with applicable federal,
state, and local laws.

title: Tack Truck/ Distributor Operator
description:
Tack Truck/ Distributor Operator
5551 Wellington Rd, Gainesville, VA, 20155, United States
Job Category
Equipment Operator, Paving Crew, Asphalt
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Description
The Tack Truck Driver is responsible for the safe and efficient operation of a tack truck
to apply asphalt emulsion in preparation for paving activities. This role supports overall
project quality by ensuring proper surface coating, maintaining equipment, and adhering
to all safety and operational standards. The position also assists paving crews with
general site support when required, contributing to smooth, coordinated, and efficient
job-site operations.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Operate a tack truck to apply asphalt emulsion uniformly and according to project
specification.
● Warms up tack material on schedule to ensure proper application temperature
and coordinates with the paving foreman and crew to achieve correct timing,
coverage, and readiness of the truck and spray system for paving operations
● Monitor material levels, spray patterns, and application rates to maintain
consistent quality
● Perform daily pre  and post trip inspections, including checking fluid levels,
hoses, pumps, and spray bars
● Clean and maintain the tack truck, spray system, and related equipment to
ensure safe and reliable operation
● Safely load, transport, and unload tack materials in compliance with company
and regulatory requirements
● Follow all traffic control procedures, safety guidelines, and environmental
regulations
● Maintain accurate logs, documentation, and daily reports as required
● Maintains a clean, safe, and compliant work environment by following all
company safety policies, adhering to OSHA regulations, and practicing safe
driving and equipment-handling procedures at all times.
● Assist with jobsite setup, including layout, traffic control, and staging of materials
when not driving.
● Other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● Valid required CDL type B with tank endorsement.
● Strong understanding of safe driving practices in active construction zones.
● 1-2 years of experience operating commercial vehicles in paving, milling, or
heavy construction environments preferred.
● Ability to read, follow and interpret safety documents, operating and maintenance
instructions and procedure manuals and plans.
● Reliable transportation and strong work ethic.
● Ability to follow verbal instructions and work as part of a team.
● Ability to adhere to Superior policies and other safety regulations.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as required.
● Must be 18 years of age
Job Competenies
To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following
competencies:
● Driving: Demonstrates the ability to safely and efficiently operate vehicles across
diverse environments
● Operating the tack truck: Demonstrates the ability to apply tack safely, accurately,
and in accordance with VDOT specifications
● Maintaining the tack truck: Demonstrates the ability to maintain safe, reliable, and
audit-ready tack truck operations.
● Safety: Maintains a strong safety record and follows safe-operation practices
● Teamwork: Collaborates openly, helps teammates, and continuously improves.
● Communication: Uses safe communication practices, reports hazards and
equipment issues, and keeps the crew informed.
Physical Demands
This position requires the ability to lift and carry up to 75 pounds; pull up one’s own
body weight; and sit in one position for up to 12 hours per day. It also requires the ability
to stand, walk, bend, kneel, and perform repetitive motions for extended periods;
tolerate noise, vibration, heat, and outdoor conditions common to paving operations;
demonstrate the manual dexterity needed to use hand tools and equipment; and
maintain good eye-hand coordination, physical fitness, multi-limb coordination, static
strength, and balance.
Work Environment/Tools and Equipment
Work is performed outdoors on roadways, construction zones, and active traffic areas,
often in hot, cold, or otherwise adverse weather. Employees are regularly exposed to
hot asphalt, dust, fumes, loud noise, and vibration from tools and equipment. Duties
require constant awareness of moving vehicles, heavy machinery, and other hazards
associated with paving operations.
The physical demands and work environment described here are representative of
those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions
of this job. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with
disabilities to perform the essential functions, in accordance with applicable federal,
state, and local laws.

title: Dump Truck Driver (Strasburg)
description:
Dump Truck Driver (Strasburg)
749 Borden Mowery Drive, Strasburg, VA, 22657, United States
Job Category
Dump Truck, Trucking
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Required Degree
NONE
Manage Others
No
Minimum Experience
2 Years
Description
This driver operates one or more types of commercial motorized vehicle dump trucks for
the purpose of hauling and dumping sand, gravel, asphalt, and other construction
materials in a regional area.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Perform pre-trip and post-trip inspections on assigned trucks to ensure safe
operating condition for each shift. This includes but not limited to, as required by
the CDL, identifying loose and/or malfunctioning parts, tires, leaks, safety
hazards, etc.
● Submits a written or electronic report on the condition of the truck at the end of
each shift.
● Operate 30-ton tri axel or 34-ton quad axel dump trucks and play knowledge of
commercial driving skills in maneuvering vehicles at varying speeds in difficult
situations, such as heavy traffic, inclement weather, or in tight loading and
unloading areas.
● Maintain vehicle and/or equipment to a high standard of cleanliness.
● Preform routine preventative maintenance and minor repairs.
● Responsible for the safety of self, others, materials, and equipment -- taking
proper safety precautions, anticipates unsafe circumstances, and acts
accordingly to prevent accidents.
● Proper loading and unloading of freight to assure safety and minimal risk of
damage to cargo and danger to people.
● Schedule and attend DOT physicals as required to maintain driving privileges.
● Maintain records required for compliance with state and federal regulations
including drivers’ logs, records of fuel purchases, mileage records, and other
records required by law.
● Promptly report any delays due to breakdowns, weather or traffic conditions,
other emergencies, or in the event of irregularities relating to pickup and delivery
of cargo.
● Report on highway safety hazards noted en-route.
● Report all accidents involving driver or company equipment.
● Perform all duties in accordance with company policies and procedures, and
comply with all federal, state, and local regulations for the safe operation of a
commercial motor vehicle.
● Report to your work assignment as scheduled, in a timely manner, while
displaying professionalism.
● Perform other duties as assigned.
Qualifications
● Valid Class B CDL, with satisfactory driving record and knowledge of commercial
driving regulations, vehicle safety, control systems, and roads is required.  H
and/or N endorsements preferred.
● 2 years’ of CDL driving experience.
● Tri-axle dump truck experience required.
● Asphalt exp. preferred.
● Must not have been cited for any DOT accidents in the past 3 years; not have
more than 3 moving violations in the past 3 years; and not have a DUI or
reckless driving violation in the last 7 years.
● Current DOT medical card and ability to pass regular DOT physicals, including
drug/alcohol screenings.
● Maintain a valid CDL in compliance with DOT regulations and advise employer of
any changes of such.
● Knowledge of DOT regulations governing safe driving, hours of service,
inspection and maintenance, and transportation of hazardous materials.
● Good communication skills required.
● Ability to read and speak English.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as necessary. Flexible - schedule
is subject to change.
● Able to work outside in all weather conditions around crews and plants.
● Ability to work as a member of a team.
● Ability to adhere to Superior policies and other safety regulations.
● Ability to read, follow and interpret safety documents, operating and maintenance
instructions and procedure manuals, plans and grade.
● Ability to follow verbal and written instructions.
● A good understanding of state, local and private requirements and regulations.
● Ability to work overtime, weekends, and nights as required.
● Must be 18 years of age
Core Responsibilities
To perform the job successfully, an individual should demonstrate the following
competencies:
● Attendance/Punctuality - is consistently at work and on time; ensures work
responsibilities are covered when absent; arrives at meetings and appointments
on time.
● Safety and Security - observes safety and security procedures; reports potentially
unsafe conditions; uses equipment and materials properly.
● Teamwork - contributes to building a positive team spirit; puts success of team
above own interests.
● Quality - demonstrates accuracy and thoroughness; monitors own work to ensure
quality.
● Productivity/Quantity - meets productivity standards; completes work in timely
manner; strives to increase productivity; works quickly.
● Dependability - follows instructions, responds to management directions;
commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals; completes tasks
on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan.
● Communication - listens and gets clarification; responds well to questions.
Physical Demands
The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met
by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable
accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the
essential functions.
Essential functions will require maintaining physical condition necessary for standing,
walking, bending, stooping, kneeling, pushing, pulling, cranking, twisting, grasping and
must occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 pounds unassisted; must be able to sit and
remain alert while driving for an aggregate period of up to 11 hours; must be able to shift
manual transmission and operate foot pedals; must be able to enter and exit the
vehicle’s cab eight to ten times a day.  Cab floor level is generally from 36 to 66 inches
above ground level, with entry and exit achieved with the assistance of various
configurations of steps (varies, possibly 3 to 5) and handholds.  Must be able to
occasionally reach above shoulder level, at waist level and below waist level for
maneuvering and directing the controls to operate the truck; operate manual tarp
covering, requiring 10 to 15 pounds of pressure, multiple times a day; seeing and
hearing within normal ranges; fine motor skills for using a computer and manual entries
into work records.
Work Environment/Tools and Equipment
The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an
employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable
accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the
essential functions.
Vehicle and field environment; travel from site to site; exposure to noise, dust, fumes,
vibrations; temperature variations and inclement weather conditions; work around heavy
construction equipment. Work on uneven or wet surfaces.
Tools and equipment regularly used; scraper, pressure washer and cleaning equipment,
hand-held jackhammer, etc.

description:
Laborer
2101 Salem Industrial Drive, Salem, VA, 24153, United States
Job Category
Paving Crew, Laborer, Asphalt
Employee Type
Non-Exempt
Description
Perform entry level maintenance and construction tasks involving physical labor at
paving sites, plant, and other contracted locations under general supervision.
Requirements
Key Responsibilities
● Clearing and preparing highway work zones, rights of ways, end of pass and
joints by removing litter, debris, rocks, existing building materials, concrete, mud
and vegetation.

established standards.
● Monitor pumps, compressors and generators.

barricades, cones, and markers, repairing pavement imperfections, marking
obstacles for equipment drivers, setting stakes and digging and backfilling
trenches and cleaning of job site.
● Other duties as may be assigned.
Qualifications

● Must be able to lift 75 lbs regularly throughout a shift
● Must be able to stand for 12 hours and be able to walk up to 4 miles in a day with
minimal breaks.
● Ability to learn how to use and handle hand tools, and control traffic according to
regulations.
● Manuel dexterity to safely use tools and equipment.
● Good eye-hand coordination, good physical fitness, multi-limb coordination, static
strength and a good sense of balance.
